title: Hormel Foods Corporation (HRL) Intrinsic Value
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Hormel Foods Corporation (HRL) compiled entirely by Gemini 3.1.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y10)
fcfGrowth.rationale: A 3.0% growth rate is estimated based on the recent free cash flow generation and the fundamental outlook for the Packaged Foods sector.

discountRate.rationale: A discount rate of 7.000000000000001% reflects the company's cost of capital, weighing systemic risks and consistent cash generation.

thesisParagraphs: Hormel Foods Corporation currently generates stable free cash flows, which are critical for its valuation in the Packaged Foods space. At a current price of $22.37, the market has embedded specific growth expectations into the stock.
Using a discounted cash flow model, we estimate an intrinsic value of $15.32. The current valuation suggests a margin of safety of -31.52%, leading to a OVERVALUED verdict.
verdictFooter: Based on actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: Why was a 3.0% growth rate used?
faq.answer: The rate aligns with normalized historical and projected fundamental growth trends in the Packaged Foods sector.

faq.question: What does the discount rate reflect?
faq.answer: The WACC represents the blended cost of equity and debt, capturing the risk profile of the cash flows.
